rationale: The cited paper is not just listed in background; the rejoinder explicitly states it is building on the commentaries, including Rao (2005), to refine its placebo framework. The text uses Rao's specific ideas (e.g., discount attribution via bargaining and motivation incentives) as concrete moderators and research questions within the model development. Rao is also revisited in the conclusion as a source of implications and agenda-setting input, indicating an active conceptual role.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Rao (2005) as a substantive commentary input to sharpen and elaborate the placebo-effects framework, especially around price-quality belief activation, attribution of discounts, and motivation-related boundary conditions. In this sense, the rejoinder refines its theorizing in response to points raised in Rao's article.
